free_text: Collectivity against nucleon transfer in sub-barrier
fusion of 12C+194,198Pt
Fusion cross sections and barrier distributions are
discussed and calculated in the framework of a
semiclassical approximation for a variety of systems.
An overall good description of the data is achieved.
Previous publication which contains data for fission
reactions and products production.  The data for
products production in A0525 differ from similar data
The data were used to
determine the mean charged-state of the beam after
passing through the target.
Nuclear Physics and Radiochemistry Divisions.
Laboratory Nazionali di Legnaro.
For the measurements, self-supporting rolled foils of
194-Pt (97% enriched and 1 mg/cm2 thick) and 198-Pt
(97% enriched and 1.3 mg/cm2 thick) were used as
targets.   The  targets were backed by Al catcher foils
of thickness 2 mg/cm2 to stop the residues.
The typical beam currents were around
20 particle nA.
The small time variation of the beam current
was taken into account for estimating evaporation
residue  cross  sections.  For improved accuracy and to
remove any interference of gamma rays arising from  any
other  source,  the  evapration residues cross sections
were deduced after following the decay for several
half-lives and using more than two gamma-ray
transitions for identifying each evapration residues.
In some cases, the cross-section values were confirmed
by following the decay of the daughter nucleus.
The low limit of the error arising from
uncertainties in branching ratio of the gamma decay and
efficiency of the hpge detector.
The high limit of the error.
The statistical error on evapration residues cross
sections is 1-3 % at higher energies and at the
lowest energies it is 5-8 %.
12-C beams were obtained from the 14UD
BARC-TIFR Pelletron.
The angular distributions for one- and
two-neutron pickup and one- and two-proton stripping
channels have been measured at 73.5 MeV.
